title: We live in public
wikitext:
[]: We live in public             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 13, 2011 12:25 am I'm not sure exactly where to put this thread, as it's not really eva but at the same time... kinda? Just saw a truely fascinating documentary entitled "We Live In Public", a biopic of an incredibly unstable dotcom millionaire who performed a number of human experiments on himself and others on a theme of privacy vs. publicity, some of which seem to me a sort of warped precursor to instrumentality in physical form. For example, right at the turn of the millenium he put a few hundred people in a bunker complex built in NY where they were filmed at all hours (and able to watch each other at will) in an atmosphere that was equal parts 1984 and burning man. It's a vision of a way of life both wonderful and terrifying simultaneously, and it's really making me rethink the whole instrumentality thing altogether. So, anybody else see this film yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Thu Oct 13, 2011 12:56 am I haven't. But it also sounds like an experiment out of the Fallout series.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 13, 2011 1:04 am That occurred to me as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Oct 13, 2011 1:28 am This sounds really interesting. I'd like to see this when I have the chance. Hopefully before this topic subsides into the forgotten so that I'll be able to share my thoughts on it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 13, 2011 1:36 am trailer

[]:             Quote Post by palehorseXIII » Thu Oct 13, 2011 4:47 am It seems to be on Netflix for anyone with a Netflix account.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Oct 13, 2011 1:04 pm As an extremely introverted person, this would be my idea of hell.

It's a vision of a way of life both wonderful and terrifying simultaneously, and it's really making me rethink the whole instrumentality thing altogether. So, anybody else see this film yet?

It's a vision of a way of life both wonderful and terrifying simultaneously, and it's really making me rethink the whole instrumentality thing altogether. So, anybody else see this film yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Sat Oct 15, 2011 12:57 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: I haven't. But it also sounds like an experiment out of the Fallout series.
